title: Intensely Nutty Turkish Fish Kofte
description: A pescatarian-friendly Turkish dish featuring pandan-leaf and salt-bake, with a tender and nutty flavor profile, perfect for a monsoon evening. This dish is a unique blend of Turkish and Asian flavors.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Soak the pandan leaves in water for 30 minutes.
Grind the almonds and walnuts in a food processor until coarsely chopped.
Mix the chopped nuts with onion, garlic, salt, and black pepper in a bowl.
Add the cod fish to the bowl and mix until just combined.
Divide the mixture into 8 equal portions and shape each portion into a kofte.
Wrap each kofte with a pandan leaf and brush with olive oil.
Place the kofte on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 12 minutes.
Serve the kofte hot with a side of salt-bake sauce.
To make the salt-bake sauce, mix 1 tablespoon of salt with 2 tablespoons of water and brush over the kofte during the last 2 minutes of baking.
